summary: Ukrainian forces struck multiple high‑value Russian logistics sites, including the Sheshharysh oil terminal, the Grousova refinery, and a shadow‑fleet tanker, while Russia reportedly prepares a new “Orjeshnik” missile strike. Drone activity remains intense across all fronts, with numerous Shahed attacks on Kyiv, Kharkiv, and southern regions. Both sides continue to exchange artillery and UAV strikes, keeping the conflict at a high operational tempo.
key_points: Ukrainian drones hit the Sheshharysh oil terminal (75 Mt/yr capacity) and Grousova refinery, igniting fires and disrupting Russian fuel supply (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
President Zelensky warned of possible Russian "Orjeshnik" missile attacks, citing intelligence from US and European partners (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Multiple Shahed attacks reported: 24 Shaheds from Kharkiv/Dnipropetrovsk to Poltava, 5 Shaheds from Kherson toward Mykolaiv, and 2 Shaheds over Kyiv region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66945).
Russian Tu‑95MS bombers (at least four) launched from Olenya airfield, indicating renewed strategic bomber operations (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66955).
Ukrainian UAV groups operating from Kherson, Donetsk, and Zaporizhzhia targeting Mykolaiv, Kharkiv, and Sumy directions (Source: @kpszsu/62522).
Russian forces attacked a college in Starobelsk, LNR, killing 21 civilians, prompting a Kremlin‑ordered counter‑strike (Source: @tass_agency/377250).
Ukrainian drones struck a Russian chemical plant “Metafrax Chemicals” near Perm, halting production of aerospace and missile components (Source: @UkraineNow/81400).
Ukrainian forces reported a successful strike on a shadow‑fleet tanker CHRYSALIS in the Black Sea (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian UAV strikes crippled key Russian fuel infrastructure, hitting the Sheshharysh terminal and Grousova refinery, while Russia readies a possible "Orjeshnik" missile attack on Kyiv. Shahed missile barrages intensified across the north and south, with dozens targeting civilian and military sites. Strategic bomber activity resumed with Tu‑95MS launches, underscoring heightened Russian air operations.

points: Ukrainian drones inflicted fires at the Sheshharysh oil terminal (capacity 75 Mt/yr) and the Grousova refinery, disrupting fuel flow to Russian forces (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
A Ukrainian UAV strike halted production at the Metafrax Chemicals plant near Perm, a key supplier of aerospace and missile components for Russia (Source: @UkraineNow/81400).
Ukrainian forces reported a successful hit on the shadow‑fleet tanker CHRYSALIS in the Black Sea, reducing Russia's maritime logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Multiple UAV groups from Kherson, Donetsk, and Zaporizhzhia moved toward Mykolaiv, Kharkiv, and Sumy, indicating coordinated deep‑strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62522).
Ukrainian intelligence, corroborated by US/EU partners, warned of a potential Russian "Orjeshnik" missile strike, prompting heightened civil defense alerts (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Russia prepared at least four Tu‑95MS strategic bombers for launch from Olenya airfield, signaling renewed long‑range bombing capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66955).
Russian forces conducted a strike on a college in Starobelsk, LNR, resulting in 21 civilian deaths, intensifying local hostilities (Source: @tass_agency/377250).
Russian air defenses engaged numerous Ukrainian UAVs across the front, with reports of downed drones in Kaluga, Rostov, and other regions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian shadow‑fleet assets, including the frigate Admiral Essen and missile boat Novorossiysk, were reported damaged by Ukrainian drones (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Continued Russian artillery and missile fire on Ukrainian cities, including reported combined‑weapon attacks on Kyiv, kept civilian casualties high (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Ukrainian UAV groups from Donetsk moved toward Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk, targeting logistics nodes (Source: @kpszsu/62522).
Shahed missiles (7 units) crossed the Kharkiv‑Poltava border heading toward Sumy, threatening supply lines (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66945).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a Russian B‑V aircraft in Poltava and Dnipropetrovsk regions during the night (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian assault aircraft advanced up to 800 m on multiple sectors of the Sumy front, indicating ongoing ground engagements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian UAVs reported over the Sumy area targeting Shostka from the north (Source: @kpszsu/62537).
Two Shahed missiles (Bandar‑L/ Geranium‑5) flew from Chernihiv to Kyiv region (Vyshhorod and Brovary districts) (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66945).
Additional Shahed attacks (2 units) over Kyiv region prompted civil defense alerts (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Ukrainian UAVs reported near Chernihiv (Makoshyne) traveling at >650 km/h, likely Geranium‑5 type (Source: @eRadarrua/93223).
Russian air defenses downed UAVs over Kaluga districts Maloyaroslavets, Borovsk, and Tarus (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian UAV group from Kherson attempted to cross into Mykolaiv but was intercepted, indicating strong northern air defense (Source: @eRadarrua/93214).
Ukrainian drones ignited fires at the Grousova oil terminal in Novorossiysk, a major Black Sea fuel hub (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Shahed missiles (5 units) from Kherson headed toward Mykolaiv, increasing pressure on southern logistics (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66945).
Ukrainian UAVs struck a shadow‑fleet tanker CHRYSALIS in the Black Sea, reducing Russian maritime capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Russian attacks damaged 8 staff buses at the ZAES transport workshop in Enerhodar, showing continued targeting of energy infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/377230).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV attacks on the Grousova oil terminal, a 1.2 Mt storage facility, causing significant fire damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81392).
Ukrainian UAVs successfully hit multiple high‑value Russian naval assets, including frigate Admiral Essen and missile boat Novorossiysk (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Russian Tu‑95MS strategic bombers launched from Olenya, indicating renewed long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66955).
Ukrainian drones downed Russian B‑V aircraft in Poltava and Dnipropetrovsk regions during night operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian air defenses shot down UAVs over Kaluga, Rostov, and other regions, reflecting high‑intensity air contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian UAV groups operated from multiple occupied territories, targeting logistics hubs across the front lines (Source: @kpszsu/62522).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones ignited fires at the Sheshharysh oil terminal (75 Mt/yr capacity) and Grousova refinery, disrupting Russian fuel logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
President Zelensky warned of a possible Russian "Orjeshnik" missile strike on Kyiv, based on US/EU intelligence (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Russian Tu‑95MS strategic bombers (minimum four) launched from Olenya, indicating renewed long‑range bombing capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66955).
Shahed missile barrages intensified: 24 Shaheds from Kharkiv/Dnipropetrovsk to Poltava, 5 from Kherson to Mykolaiv, and multiple attacks over Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66945).
Ukrainian UAVs struck the shadow‑fleet tanker CHRYSALIS in the Black Sea, reducing Russian maritime logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81403).
Russian attack on a college in Starobelsk, LNR killed 21 civilians, prompting a Kremlin counter‑strike (Source: @tass_agency/377250).
Ukrainian drones halted production at the Metafrax Chemicals plant near Perm, a key supplier of aerospace and missile components for Russia (Source: @UkraineNow/81400).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian preparations for a novel "Orjeshnik" missile strike, combined with renewed Tu‑95MS bomber sorties, suggest an escalation toward long‑range precision attacks on Ukrainian urban centers. Ukraine's aggressive UAV campaign continues to degrade Russian logistics, especially fuel and maritime assets, but the high volume of Shahed missiles threatens both military and civilian infrastructure across the north and south. Both sides are sustaining high operational tempos, increasing the risk of broader escalation and civilian casualties.
sections.regional_response: Western partners have intensified intelligence sharing, as evidenced by Zelensky's citation of US/EU data on the "Orjeshnik" threat, while also supporting Ukraine's air defense upgrades. Russia, facing mounting logistical losses, appears to rely on strategic bombers and missile strikes to compensate, maintaining pressure on critical Ukrainian infrastructure. Local authorities in Kyiv and southern regions have heightened civil defense alerts, reflecting the growing threat to civilian populations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of Shahed launch sites in Chernihiv, Kharkiv, and Kherson is essential, given their frequent cross‑border trajectories. UAV activity from occupied territories indicates a coordinated command structure capable of deep strikes; SIGINT should focus on command‑and‑control nodes in Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia. The reported Russian bomber deployments from Olenya warrant satellite surveillance to assess sortie rates and potential target lists.
